steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a tall vertical line — the axis of the vase. Symmetric vessels live or die by their axis, so take your time getting it straight.

steps.title: Block the silhouette (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch the vase profile around the axis: a wide mouth at the top, a slender neck below it, a fuller belly, and a narrow foot. Mirror each curve evenly across the axis.

steps.title: Mark the rim and base (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, add a shallow ellipse along the rim opening and a narrower one at the foot. These describe the round cross-section and stop the vase from reading as a flat cutout.

steps.title: Plan the flowers (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch two or three stems rising from the mouth of the vase, each leaning at a slightly different angle. Mark a small circle at the top of each stem for where a bloom will sit.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the vase
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the vase silhouette with confident, flowing strokes — let each side feel like one continuous gesture. Trace the front of the rim ellipse cleanly; stop where the stems will cover it.

steps.title: Ink the stems (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw each stem rising out of the rim. Where stems cross in front of each other, stop the back line cleanly where the front crosses — that little gap is what tells the eye one is in front of the other.

steps.title: Ink the blooms (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw a bloom at the top of each stem. Vary them — one might be a tight tulip cup, another a looser daisy-like cluster of petals. Add a small leaf or two along the stems.
